---
title: "Simulation_Full"
output: html_document
date: "2026-03-14"
---

# Libraries
```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(spatstat.geom)
library(spatstat.random)
library(spatstat.explore)
library(SpatialExperiment)
```

# OKAY LET'S START BY WRITING THE FUNCTIONS THAT WE'LL NEED

```{r}
# --------------------------------------------------
# TREE_MAP
#
# Purpose:
# This table defines the biological hierarchy for each
# terminal cell subtype in the simulation.
#
# Idea:
# Each simulated cell is usually stored using its most
# specific label only, e.g. "CD4_T" or "Naive_B".
#
# This mapping table tells us which larger families that
# subtype belongs to.
#
# For example:
#   CD4_T -> T -> Lymphoid -> Immune -> Hematopoietic
#
# Why this is useful:
# 1. It avoids repeating the full lineage in every row
#    of the simulated dataset.
# 2. It lets us recover higher-level groupings later
#    using a join.
# 3. It helps define the hierarchy used for multiple
#    testing methods such as TreeBH, DART, and p-filter.
#
# How to use it:
# Suppose your simulated cells look like this:
#   x, y, cellType_sub
#
# Then you can attach the hierarchy with:
#
#   cells <- cells %>%
#     left_join(TREE_MAP, by = c("cellType_sub" = "subtype"))
#
# After joining, each cell can also carry:
TREE_MAP <- tribble(
  ~subtype,      ~major,      ~lineage,
  "Naive_B",     "B",         "Lymphoid",
  "Memory_B",    "B",         "Lymphoid",
  "CD4_T",       "T",         "Lymphoid",
  "CD8_T",       "T",         "Lymphoid",
  "Memory_T",    "T",         "Lymphoid",
  "NK",          "NK",        "Lymphoid",
  "Monocyte",    "Monocyte",  "Myeloid",
  "M1_like",     "Macrophage","Myeloid",
  "M2_like",     "Macrophage","Myeloid",
  "Neutrophil",  "Granulocyte","Myeloid",
  "Eosinophil",  "Granulocyte","Myeloid",
  "Basophil",    "Granulocyte","Myeloid",
  "cDC1",        "Dendritic", "Myeloid",
  "cDC2",        "Dendritic", "Myeloid",
  "pDC",         "Dendritic", "Myeloid",
  "Mast",        "Mast",      "Myeloid",
  "Fibroblast",  "Stromal",   "Stromal",
  "Endothelial", "Stromal",   "Stromal",
  "Pericyte",    "Stromal",   "Stromal",
  "Epithelial",  "Tissue",    "Tissue"
)

# --------------------------------------------------
# normalize_im()
#
# Purpose:
# Convert a spatial image into a proper probability density
# so that it can be used safely in rpoint().
# --------------------------------------------------
normalize_im <- function(im_obj) {
  total_mass <- integral.im(im_obj)
  
  if (!is.finite(total_mass) || total_mass <= 0) {
    stop("Image cannot be normalized because total mass is not positive.")
  }
  
  im_obj / total_mass
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# label_cells()
#
# Purpose:
# Convert a spatstat point pattern into a tibble and
# assign a leaf cell type label.
#
# Inputs:
#   pp           = spatstat point pattern (ppp object)
#   cell_subtype = most specific cell label
#
# Output:
#   tibble with x, y, cellType_sub
# --------------------------------------------------
label_cells <- function(pp, cell_subtype) {
  if (!inherits(pp, "ppp")) {
    stop("pp must be a spatstat point pattern object of class 'ppp'.")
  }
  
  tibble(
    x = pp$x,
    y = pp$y,
    cellType_sub = cell_subtype
  )
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# add_tree_labels()
#
# Purpose:
# Use TREE_MAP to recover broader hierarchy labels
# from the leaf subtype label.
#
# Inputs:
#   cells    = tibble with a column called cellType_sub
#   tree_map = lookup table mapping subtype to hierarchy
#
# Output:
#   tibble with hierarchy columns added
# --------------------------------------------------
add_tree_labels <- function(cells, tree_map = TREE_MAP) {
  if (!"cellType_sub" %in% colnames(cells)) {
    stop("cells must contain a column called 'cellType_sub'.")
  }
  
  out <- cells %>%
    left_join(tree_map, by = c("cellType_sub" = "subtype"))
  
  missing_types <- out %>%
    filter(is.na(major) | is.na(lineage)) %>%
    distinct(cellType_sub) %>%
    pull(cellType_sub)
  
  if (length(missing_types) > 0) {
    stop(
      paste(
        "These subtypes are missing from TREE_MAP:",
        paste(missing_types, collapse = ", ")
      )
    )
  }
  
  out
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# simulate_cells_from_density()
#
# Purpose:
# Simulate n cells from a spatial density and label them
# with a leaf subtype.
#
# Inputs:
#   n            = number of cells
#   cell_subtype = leaf cell label
#   density      = spatstat image
#   win          = simulation window
#
# Output:
#   tibble with x, y, cellType_sub
# --------------------------------------------------
simulate_cells_from_density <- function(n, cell_subtype, density, win) {
  if (n <= 0) return(NULL)
  
  d <- normalize_im(density)
  pp <- rpoint(n = n, f = d, win = win)
  
  label_cells(pp, cell_subtype)
}

# --------------------------------------------------
# simulate_mixture_cells()
#
# Purpose:
# Simulate n cells from a mixture of two densities and
# label them with a leaf subtype.
#
# Inputs:
#   n            = number of cells
#   cell_subtype = leaf cell label
#   density1     = first density
#   density2     = second density
#   weight       = mixing weight in [0, 1]
#   win          = simulation window
#
# Output:
#   tibble with x, y, cellType_sub
# --------------------------------------------------
simulate_mixture_cells <- function(n, cell_subtype,
                                   density1, density2, weight, win) {
  if (n <= 0) return(NULL)
  
  if (!is.numeric(weight) || length(weight) != 1 || weight < 0 || weight > 1) {
    stop("weight must be a single number between 0 and 1.")
  }
  
  d1 <- normalize_im(density1)
  d2 <- normalize_im(density2)
  
  mixed_density <- (1 - weight) * d1 + weight * d2
  pp <- rpoint(n = n, f = mixed_density, win = win)
  
  label_cells(pp, cell_subtype)
}

# -------------------------------------------------- 
# simulate_pp() 
# 
# Purpose: 
# Simulate a completely spatially random point pattern 
# inside a given window.
# 
# Idea: 
# This uses a homogeneous Poisson point process, meaning: 
# - points are placed independently of one another 
# - every location in the window is equally likely 
# - there is no clustering or repulsion 
# 
# The intensity is chosen so that the expected total number # of points in the window is n. 
# 
# Inputs: 
# n = desired expected number of points 
# win = spatstat window object defining the simulation region 
# 
# Output: 
# A tibble with one row per simulated point, containing: 
# x, y = simulated spatial coordinates 
# 
# Notes: 
# - The number of simulated points is random, not fixed exactly at n 
# - The actual number of points is Poisson distributed with mean n 
# - This is a useful null model for complete spatial randomness 
# -------------------------------------------------- 

simulate_pp <- function(n, win) { 
  rpoispp(n / area.owin(win), win = win) 
}

```

# Let us Begin

```{text}
Heimatopetic
├── Immune
│   ├── Lymphoid
│   │   ├── B
│   │   │   ├── Naive_B
│   │   │   └── Memory_B
│   │   │ 
│   │   ├── T
│   │   │   ├── CD4_T
│   │   │   ├── CD8_T
│   │   │   └── Memory_T
│   │   │   
│   │   └── NK
│   └── Myeloid
│       ├── Monocyte
│       ├── Macrophage
│       │   ├── M1_like
│       │   └── M2_like
│       ├── Granulocyte
│       │   ├── Neutrophil
│       │   ├── Eosinophil
│       │   └── Basophil
│       ├── Dendritic
│       │   ├── cDC1
│       │   ├── cDC2
│       │   └── pDC
│       └── Mast
├── Stromal
│   ├── Fibroblast
│   ├── Endothelial
│   └── Pericyte
└── Tissue
    └── Epithelial
```

```{r}
# Setting up the window
WIN <- owin(c(0, 1000), c(0, 1000))

# Main Density
main_pp <- simulate_pp(1000, WIN)
main_density <- density.ppp(main_pp, bw.diggle(main_pp))

```

## Immune Layer
```{r}
immune_pp <- rpoint(n = 250, f = normalize_im(main_density), win = WIN)
immune_density <- density.ppp(immune_pp, bw.diggle(immune_pp))

# Lympoid Density
lymphoid_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(immune_density), win = WIN)
lymphoid_density <- density.ppp(lymphoid_pp, bw.diggle(lymphoid_pp))

# Myeloid Density
myeloid_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(immune_density), win = WIN)
myeloid_density <- density.ppp(myeloid_pp, bw.diggle(myeloid_pp))
```
### Lymphoid Branch
  
```{r}
# ===================================
# Lymphoid Branch
# ===================================

# B-Cell Branch
b_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(lymphoid_density), win=WIN)
b_density <- density.ppp(b_pp, bw.diggle(b_pp))

naive_b_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Naive_B",
  density = b_density,
  win = WIN
)

memory_b_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Memory_B",
  density = b_density,
  win = WIN
)

# T-Cell Branch
t_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(lymphoid_density), win=WIN)
t_density <- density.ppp(t_pp, bw.diggle(t_pp))

CD4_t_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "CD4_T",
  density = t_density,
  win = WIN
)

CD8_t_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "CD8_T",
  density = t_density,
  win = WIN
)

memory_t_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Memory_T",
  density = t_density,
  win = WIN
)

# NK-Cell Branch
nk_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(lymphoid_density), win = WIN)
nk_density <- density.ppp(nk_pp, bw.diggle(nk_pp))

nk_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "NK",
  density = nk_density,
  win = WIN
)

```

### Myeloid Branch

```{r}
# ===================================
# Myeloid Branch
# ===================================

# Monocyte Branch
monocyte_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(myeloid_density), win = WIN)
monocyte_density <- density.ppp(monocyte_pp, bw.diggle(monocyte_pp))

monocyte_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Monocyte",
  density = monocyte_density,
  win = WIN
)


# Macrophage Branch
macrophage_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(myeloid_density), win = WIN)
macrophage_density <- density.ppp(macrophage_pp, bw.diggle(macrophage_pp))

m1_like_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "M1_like",
  density = macrophage_density,
  win = WIN
)

m2_like_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "M2_like",
  density = macrophage_density,
  win = WIN
)


# Granulocyte Branch
granulocyte_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(myeloid_density), win = WIN)
granulocyte_density <- density.ppp(granulocyte_pp, bw.diggle(granulocyte_pp))

neutrophil_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Neutrophil",
  density = granulocyte_density,
  win = WIN
)

eosinophil_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Eosinophil",
  density = granulocyte_density,
  win = WIN
)

basophil_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Basophil",
  density = granulocyte_density,
  win = WIN
)


# Dendritic Branch
dendritic_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(myeloid_density), win = WIN)
dendritic_density <- density.ppp(dendritic_pp, bw.diggle(dendritic_pp))

cdc1_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "cDC1",
  density = dendritic_density,
  win = WIN
)

cdc2_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "cDC2",
  density = dendritic_density,
  win = WIN
)

pdc_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "pDC",
  density = dendritic_density,
  win = WIN
)


# Mast Branch
mast_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(myeloid_density), win = WIN)
mast_density <- density.ppp(mast_pp, bw.diggle(mast_pp))

mast_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Mast",
  density = mast_density,
  win = WIN
)
```


## Stromal Layer

```{r}
# Stromal Layer
stromal_pp <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(main_density), win = WIN)
stromal_density <- density.ppp(stromal_pp, bw.diggle(stromal_pp))

# Fibroblast Cells
fibroblast_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n=150,
  cell_subtype = "Fibroblast",
  density = stromal_density,
  win = WIN
)

# Endothelial Cells
endothelial_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n=150,
  cell_subtype = "Endothelial",
  density = stromal_density,
  win = WIN
)

# Pericyte Cells
pericyte_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n=150,
  cell_subtype = "Pericyte",
  density = stromal_density,
  win = WIN
)

# Combining them all
stromal_all <- bind_rows(
  fibroblast_cells,
  endothelial_cells,
  pericyte_cells
)
```


## Tissue Layer

```{r}
# Tissue Layer
tissue_pp  <- rpoint(n = 100, f = normalize_im(main_density), win = WIN)
tissue_density <- density.ppp(tissue_pp, bw.diggle(tissue_pp))

# Epithelial Cells
epithelial_cells <- simulate_cells_from_density(
  n = 150,
  cell_subtype = "Epithelial",
  density = tissue_density,
  win = WIN
)

tissue_all <- bind_rows(
  epithelial_cells
)
```

# Tie Things Together

```{r}
lymphoid_all <- bind_rows(
  naive_b_cells,
  memory_b_cells,
  CD4_t_cells,
  CD8_t_cells,
  memory_t_cells
)

myeloid_all <- bind_rows(
  monocyte_cells,
  m1_like_cells,
  m2_like_cells,
  neutrophil_cells,
  eosinophil_cells,
  basophil_cells,
  cdc1_cells,
  cdc2_cells,
  pdc_cells,
  mast_cells
)

immune_all <- bind_rows(
  lymphoid_all,
  myeloid_all
)

all_cells <- bind_rows(
  immune_all,
  stromal_all,
  tissue_all
) %>%
  add_tree_labels() %>%
  mutate(
    sample_id = "sample_1",
    condition = "control"
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(all_cells, aes(x = x, y = y, colour = cellType_sub)) +
  geom_point(size = 0.8, alpha = 0.7) +
  coord_equal() +
  theme_bw()
```
